metadata.name: coreapi
metadata.summary: Python client library for Core API.

functions.name: coreapi.utils.domain_matches
documentation:
Domain string matching against an outgoing request.
Patterns starting with '*' indicate a wildcard domain.

functions.name: coreapi.utils.guess_extension
documentation:
Python's `mimetypes.guess_extension` is no use because it simply returns
the first of an unordered set. We use the same set of media types here,
but take a reasonable preference on what extension to map to.

functions.name: coreapi.utils.determine_transport
documentation: Given a URL determine the appropriate transport instance.
Negotiation utilities. USed to determine which codec or transport class
should be used, given a list of supported instances.

functions.name: coreapi.utils.negotiate_decoder
documentation:
Given the value of a 'Content-Type' header, return the appropriate
codec for decoding the request content.

functions.name: coreapi.utils.negotiate_encoder
documentation:
Given the value of a 'Accept' header, return the appropriate codec for
encoding the response content.

functions.name: coreapi.utils.validate_path_param
documentation: Validation utilities. Used to ensure that we get consistent validation
exceptions when invalid types are passed as a parameter, rather than
an exception occuring when the request is made.

functions.name: coreapi.utils._validate_form_object
documentation: Ensure that `value` can be encoded as form data or as query parameters.

functions.name: coreapi.utils._validate_form_field
documentation:
Ensure that `value` can be encoded as a single form data or a query parameter.
Basic types that has a simple string representation are supported.
A list of basic types is also valid.

functions.name: coreapi.utils._validate_json_data
documentation: Ensure that `value` can be encoded into JSON.

classes.name: coreapi.utils.DownloadedFile
documentation: Ideally we subclass this so that we can present a custom representation.

modules.name: coreapi.exceptions
documentation: coding: utf-8

classes.name: coreapi.exceptions.CoreAPIException
documentation: A base class for all `coreapi` exceptions.

classes.name: coreapi.exceptions.ParseError
documentation: Raised when an invalid Core API encoding is encountered.

classes.name: coreapi.exceptions.NoCodecAvailable
documentation: Raised when there is no available codec that can handle the given media.

classes.name: coreapi.exceptions.NetworkError
documentation: Raised when the transport layer fails to make a request or get a response.

classes.name: coreapi.exceptions.LinkLookupError
documentation: Raised when `.action` fails to index a link in the document.

classes.name: coreapi.exceptions.ParameterError
documentation:
Raised when the parameters passed do not match the link fields.

* A required field was not included.
* An unknown field was included.
* A field was passed an invalid type for the link location/encoding.

classes.name: coreapi.exceptions.ErrorMessage
documentation: Raised when the transition returns an error message.

modules.name: coreapi.codecs.python
documentation: Note that `DisplayCodec` is deliberately omitted from the documentation,
as it is considered an implementation detail.
It may move into a utility function in the future.

classes.name: coreapi.codecs.python.PythonCodec
documentation: A Python representation of a Document, for use with '__repr__'.

modules.name: coreapi.codecs.jsondata
documentation: coding: utf-8

methods.name: coreapi.codecs.jsondata.JSONCodec.decode
documentation: Return raw JSON data.

modules.name: coreapi.codecs.text
documentation: coding: utf-8

functions.name: coreapi.client._lookup_link
documentation:
Validates that keys looking up a link are correct.

Returns a two-tuple of (link, link_ancestors).

functions.name: coreapi.client._validate_parameters
documentation:
Ensure that parameters passed to the link are correct.
Raises a `ParameterError` if any parameters do not validate.

modules.name: coreapi.codecs
documentation: coding: utf-8

methods.name: coreapi.codecs.base.BaseCodec.dump
documentation: We don't implement stubs, to ensure that we can check which of these
two operations a codec supports. For example:
`if hasattr(codec, 'decode'): ...`
def decode(self, bytestring, **options):
pass
def encode(self, document, **options):
pass
The following will be removed at some point, most likely in a 2.1 release:

modules.name: coreapi
documentation: coding: utf-8

modules.name: coreapi.codecs.download
documentation: coding: utf-8

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.download._unique_output_path
documentation:
Given a path like '/a/b/c.txt'

Return the first available filename that doesn't already exist,
using an incrementing suffix if needed.

For example: '/a/b/c.txt' or '/a/b/c (1).txt' or '/a/b/c (2).txt'...

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.download._safe_filename
documentation: Sanitize output filenames, to remove any potentially unsafe characters.

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.download._get_filename_from_content_disposition
documentation: Determine an output filename based on the `Content-Disposition` header.

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.download._get_filename_from_url
documentation: Determine an output filename based on the download URL.

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.download._get_filename
documentation: Determine an output filename to use for the download.

classes.name: coreapi.codecs.download.DownloadCodec

methods.name: coreapi.codecs.download.DownloadCodec.__init__
documentation: `download_dir` - The path to use for file downloads.
A codec to handle raw file downloads, such as images and other media.

modules.name: coreapi.codecs.display
documentation: Note that `DisplayCodec` is deliberately omitted from the documentation,
as it is considered an implementation detail.
It may move into a utility function in the future.

classes.name: coreapi.codecs.display.DisplayCodec
documentation: A plaintext representation of a Document, intended for readability.

modules.name: coreapi.compat
documentation: coding: utf-8

methods.name: coreapi.auth.TokenAuthentication.__init__
documentation:
* Use an unauthenticated client, and make a request to obtain a token.
* Create an authenticated client using eg. `TokenAuthentication(token="<token>")`

classes.name: coreapi.auth.SessionAuthentication
documentation:
Enables session based login.

* Make an initial request to obtain a CSRF token.
* Make a login request.

modules.name: coreapi.transports
documentation: coding: utf-8

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.corejson._get_schema
documentation: Robust dictionary lookups, that always return an item of the correct
type, using an empty default if an incorrect type exists.
Useful for liberal parsing of inputs.

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.corejson._graceful_relative_url
documentation:
Return a graceful link for a URL relative to a base URL.

* If they are the same, return an empty string.
* If the have the same scheme and hostname, return the path & query params.
* Otherwise return the full URL.

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.corejson._escape_key
documentation:
The '_type' and '_meta' keys are reserved.
Prefix with an additional '_' if they occur.

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.corejson._unescape_key
documentation: Unescape '__type' and '__meta' keys if they occur.

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.corejson._get_content
documentation: Return a dictionary of content, for documents, objects and errors.

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.corejson._document_to_primitive
documentation:
Take a Core API document and return Python primitives
ready to be rendered into the JSON style encoding.

functions.name: coreapi.codecs.corejson._primitive_to_document
documentation:
Take Python primitives as returned from parsing JSON content,
and return a Core API document.

methods.name: coreapi.codecs.corejson.CoreJSONCodec.decode
documentation: Takes a bytestring and returns a document.

methods.name: coreapi.codecs.corejson.CoreJSONCodec.encode
documentation: Takes a document and returns a bytestring.

modules.name: coreapi.transports.base
documentation: coding: utf-8

modules.name: coreapi.document
documentation: coding: utf-8

functions.name: coreapi.document._key_sorting
documentation:
Document and Object sorting.
Regular attributes sorted alphabetically.
Links are sorted based on their URL and action.

classes.name: coreapi.document.Document
documentation:
The Core API document type.

Expresses the data that the client may access,
and the actions that the client may perform.
The Core API primitives:

classes.name: coreapi.document.Object
documentation: An immutable mapping of strings to values.

classes.name: coreapi.document.Array
documentation: An immutable list type container.

classes.name: coreapi.document.Link
documentation: Links represent the actions that a client may perform.

modules.name: coreapi.transports.http
documentation: coding: utf-8

functions.name: coreapi.transports.http._get_params
documentation: Separate the params into the various types.

functions.name: coreapi.transports.http._get_url
documentation:
Given a templated URL and some parameters that have been provided,
expand the URL.

functions.name: coreapi.transports.http._get_headers
documentation: Return a dictionary of HTTP headers to use in the outgoing request.

functions.name: coreapi.transports.http._get_upload_headers
documentation:
When a raw file upload is made, determine the Content-Type and
Content-Disposition headers to use with the request.

functions.name: coreapi.transports.http._build_http_request
documentation: Make an HTTP request and return an HTTP response.

functions.name: coreapi.transports.http._coerce_to_error_content
documentation:
Errors should not contain nested documents or links.
If we get a 4xx or 5xx response with a Document, then coerce
the document content into plain data.

functions.name: coreapi.transports.http._coerce_to_error
documentation: Given an arbitrary return result, coerce it into an Error instance.

functions.name: coreapi.transports.http._decode_result
documentation: Given an HTTP response, return the decoded Core API document.

functions.name: coreapi.transports.http._handle_inplace_replacements
documentation:
Given a new document, and the link/ancestors it was created,
determine if we should:

* Make an inline replacement and then return the modified document tree.
* Return the new document as-is.

classes.name: coreapi.transports.http.ForceMultiPartDict
documentation:
A dictionary that always evaluates as True.
Allows us to force requests to use multipart encoding, even when no
file parameters are passed.

classes.name: coreapi.transports.http.BlockAll
documentation:
A cookie policy that rejects all cookies.
Used to override the default `requests` behavior.

classes.name: coreapi.transports.http.DomainCredentials
documentation: Custom auth class to support deprecated 'credentials' argument.

classes.name: coreapi.transports.http.CallbackAdapter
documentation: Custom requests HTTP adapter, to support deprecated callback arguments.
